data.title: Root cause: JWT token expiry race between refresh worker and request handler
data.description:
## Summary

Identified the root cause of the intermittent 401s tracked in **OPS-355**: a **JWT token expiry race condition** inside **auth-service**.

## Root cause

auth-service runs two independent code paths that both evaluate token expiry without coordination:

1. **Request handler** — validates the bearer token on `/v1/introspect` and API calls
2. **Background refresh worker** — proactively rotates refresh tokens nearing expiry

Under concurrent load both paths can observe the same session within a narrow window (~50–120ms). The worker rotates the refresh token and invalidates the prior access token while an in-flight request still presents the old token. The handler rejects it with **401 Unauthorized** even though the client believed the token was valid milliseconds earlier.

There is no shared lock or single source of truth for "token generation epoch" across the worker and handler.

## Related

- Parent / symptom ticket: **OPS-355**

## Fix direction

- Serialize refresh via Redis distributed lock keyed on `session_id`
- Introduce monotonic `token_version` checked atomically in both paths
- Add integration test reproducing concurrent refresh + introspect

## Owners

- @maya-chen (auth-service lead)
- @jordan-park (SRE on-call)

issue.title: Intermittent 401 errors from auth-service under concurrent load
